evidence_excerpt: CT abdomen/pelvis concerning for biliary tract malignancy with hepatic masses and possible portal vein occlusion; family chose comfort-focused care. Patient was discharged with a hospice comfort medication bundle, indicating transition to an irreversible terminal state.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Presented with severe abdominal pain; discharged on hospice comfort medications including concentrated oral morphine PRN q1h for pain, olanzapine for delirium/restlessness, and scopolamine for secretions.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: MRI head showed punctate new acute/subacute infarcts in the right frontal and parietal lobes.
Decision to focus on comfort care and discharge with hospice medication bundle due to likely cholangiocarcinoma with hepatic masses and omental caking.
Discharge exam noted the patient 'appears comfortable'; pain managed with morphine and supportive measures.

notes_on_uncertainty: No post-discharge follow-up is provided; death is inferred from transition to hospice as an irreversible terminal state. Long-term functional and symptom assessments are based on discharge status and comfort-focused plan.
